raw_results.title: Open Knowledge Format | Definition, Scope and Compliance (Grounding Page)
raw_results.content: This page serves as a structured reference for the specification Open Knowledge Format (OKF). The **Open Knowledge Format** is an open specification (version 0.1) published by Google Cloud on 12 June 2026. It represents knowledge as a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter, so that metadata and curated context can be authored, exchanged and consumed by both humans and AI agents. This Grounding Page defines a single normative specification: the Open Knowledge Format, version 0.1, as published in the GoogleCloudPlatform knowledge-catalog repository. The Open Knowledge Format is an open specification that represents a body of knowledge as a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter, designed to be authored by people, generated by agents and consumed by both. The unit of knowledge inside a bundle is a concept, represented as one markdown document. The Open Knowledge Format is an open, vendor-neutral specification published by Google Cloud that represents knowledge as a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter.

raw_results.title: Google's Open Knowledge Format: The Markdown Standard That Could Replace Your Wiki | Flowtivity
raw_results.content: # Google's Open Knowledge Format: The Markdown Standard That Could Replace Your Wiki. Google just formalized the digital brain pattern — plain markdown files that AI agents can read, write, and share. Here's what OKF means for your business and why it could replace Notion or Obsidian for technical teams. Here's what it means for your business and why it matters more than you think.*. The Open Knowledge Format (OKF) is an open, vendor-neutral specification for storing organizational knowledge as a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Announced by Google Cloud on June 12, 2026, OKF gives AI agents a standardized way to read, write, and exchange knowledge without proprietary platforms, SDKs, or lock-in. Each "concept" is a single markdown file. Files interlink with normal markdown links, creating a living wiki that both humans and AI agents can navigate. **An OKF bundle is a directory of markdown files.** Each file represents one "concept" — a table, an API endpoint, a metric, a playbook, anything you want to capture.

Google Cloud shipped the Open Knowledge Format: portable markdown files that let AI agents read curated knowledge. The Open Knowledge Format: one portable file format your brand's knowledge can live in. On 12 June 2026, Google Cloud published the Open Knowledge Format (OKF), an open spec for sharing curated knowledge with AI agents as plain markdown files. Read closely, it is also a blueprint for marketers: your brand's knowledge belongs in portable, agent-readable files the team controls, so any model can read it. This is how to read a data-engineering standard as a marketing playbook. The Open Knowledge Format is an open spec, published by Google Cloud on 12 June 2026, for representing curated knowledge as a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter. OKF gives each concept a plain-language file an agent can read, published as an open standard.

raw_results.title: What is Open Knowledge Format(OKF)?
raw_results.content: Shoutout to Google Cloud Data Cloud. **TLDR:**Open Knowledge Format is a vendor-neutral open spec for AI context. Use markdown and YAML to build portable interoperable agent knowledge bases. **Read the following first:**_What is LLM Wiki Pattern? The feeling of having all the information needed but finding it scattered across ten different places is familiar to anyone working with AI agents today. The Google Cloud Data Team has observed this problem up close. Every team building with AI runs into the same wall. That context is scattered across catalogs, wikis, code comments, and the heads of a few senior engineers. Teams are discovering that the best way to give AI agents context is a wiki. Markdown files that humans can read and agents can parse. The Google Cloud Data Team is introducing the Open Knowledge Format. It is a specification that formalizes the LLM wiki pattern into something portable and interoperable. An OKF bundle is a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter.

The Open Knowledge Format is an open, vendor-neutral specification published by Google Cloud that represents knowledge as a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter.

Google just formalized the digital brain pattern — plain markdown files that AI agents can read, write, and share. The Open Knowledge Format (OKF) is an open, vendor-neutral specification for storing organizational knowledge as a directory of markdown files with YAML frontmatter. Announced by Google Cloud on June 12, 2026, OKF gives AI agents a standardized way to read, write, and exchange knowledge without proprietary platforms, SDKs, or lock-in. Each "concept" is a single markdown file. - Designed for curated context and portable, interoperable knowledge bases—particularly useful for teams working with AI agents.

## Important Findings
- OKF is an open specification published by Google Cloud for representing knowledge as directories of markdown files with YAML frontmatter. (S1)
